single_file.ToonPlayerDescMap_behavior: STRUCT with dynamic player-ID keys — each file gets a unique struct schema. Single-file: STRUCT("<toon-id>" STRUCT(...)). Prevents cross-file union without union_by_name.
single_file.notes: All 11 root keys successfully parsed. Event arrays become typed STRUCT[] arrays.

batch_with_union_by_name.note: With union_by_name=true, DuckDB correctly promotes ToonPlayerDescMap to MAP type, resolving the dynamic-key problem.

event_array_storage_estimate.fits_on_local_ssd: Marginal — 367.6 GB as JSON columns would consume substantial SSD space. DuckDB compression will reduce this, but the uncompressed estimate is a concern.

batch_ingestion_test.memory_behavior: Completed within 24GB memory limit without issues

proposed_table_split_strategy.rationale: Event arrays (gameEvents, trackerEvents) dominate file size (~95% of bytes). Single-table approach would create ~370 GB of JSON columns that are rarely queried together with metadata. Split into: (1) replays_metadata_raw: header, details, metadata, initData, ToonPlayerDescMap, error flags, filename; (2) replay_events_raw: gameEvents, trackerEvents, messageEvents, filename — or load events on-demand per analysis.

recommended_ddl.replays_metadata_raw: CREATE TABLE replays_metadata_raw AS SELECT header, details, metadata, initData, ToonPlayerDescMap, gameEventsErr, messageEventsErr, trackerEvtsErr, filename FROM read_json_auto('raw/*/_data/*.SC2Replay.json', union_by_name=true, filename=true, maximum_object_size=536870912)
recommended_ddl.replay_game_events_raw: Deferred — load on-demand due to 327 GB estimated size
recommended_ddl.replay_tracker_events_raw: Deferred — load on-demand due to 41 GB estimated size

proposed_table_split_strategy.filename_column_requirement: Every raw-layer table MUST include filename=true for provenance tracing.

structure.pattern: foreign map name -> English map name

mapping_file_census.proposed_table_ddl: CREATE TABLE map_aliases_raw AS SELECT unnest(map_keys(json)) AS foreign_name, unnest(map_values(json)) AS english_name, filename FROM read_json_auto('raw/*/map_foreign_to_english_mapping.json', filename=true) — produces 1,488 * 70 = 104,160 rows. Alternatively, since all 70 files are identical, load just one.
